counterfactual_model.summary: Without the top-up, sustained rate limiting and failure pressure would likely have forced manual operator intervention before a formal incident trigger escalated into a recovery workflow.
assumptions: Primary continuity value in month 1 is labor avoided, not SLA credits.
A rate-limit/failure pattern above trigger threshold would have required founder/on-call intervention within the claim window.
No downtime revenue-loss claims are counted in month 1 decisioning.

post_event_verification.trend_summary: Combined failure rate fell below the 5% trigger threshold and remained below threshold for the remainder of the claim window.

decision_status.notes: Illustrative example only. Replace placeholder IDs, tx hash, and metrics with real production telemetry before use. In month 1, if any Tier 1/Tier 2 amount is only weakly inferred rather than fully evidenced, move that amount into unproven_estimate_usd and exclude it from decisioning. Conservative proven claims in the $400-$600 range may be more appropriate until 2-3 cycles establish evidence quality.
